channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Singapore rushes to clean-up oil slick after boat hits stationary fuel supply ship
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — An oil spill caused by a dredger boat hitting a stationary cargo tanker has blackened part of Singapore’s southern coastline, including the popular resort island of Sentosa, and sparked concerns it may threaten marine wildlife as a clean-up operation was underway Sunday
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italian PM Meloni gives France's Macron 'death stare' after clash over G-7 statement
item.description: Italian Prime Minister looked not too thrilled in a viral video showing her shaking the hand of French President Emmanuel Macron during the G-7 summit in Italy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Divers find remains of Finnish WWII plane that was shot down by Moscow with a US diplomat aboard
item.description: The plane was carrying American and French diplomatic couriers in June 1940 when it was downed just days before Moscow annexed the Baltic states.
item.dc:creator: JARI TANNER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sweden frees an Iranian man convicted over 1988 mass executions in exchange for 2 men held by Iran
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran and Sweden carried out a prisoner swap Saturday that saw Tehran release a European Union diplomat and another man in exchange for an Iranian convicted in Stockholm of committing war crimes over his part in 1988 mass executions in the Islamic Republic.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran and Sweden to swap prisoners, including convicted war criminal
item.description: Iran agreed Saturday to release two Swedish nationals held in Tehran for over two years in exchange for a convicted Iranian war criminal tied to the 1988 mass killing of protestors critical of the Islamic Republic in the capital city.
item.dc:creator: Yash Roy, The Hill

item.title: Honduras plans to build a 20,000-capacity 'megaprison' for gang members as part of a crackdown
item.description: TEGUCIGALPA, Honduras (AP) — The president of Honduras has announced the creation of a new 20,000-capacity “megaprison," part of the government's larger crackdown on gang violence and efforts to overhaul its long-troubled prison system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World leaders meet in Switzerland to discuss a Ukraine peace roadmap. Russia is notably absent
item.description: OBBÜRGEN, Switzerland (AP) — Dozens of world leaders converged on a Swiss resort Saturday to discuss how to bring peace to war-ravaged Ukraine, though any hopes of a real breakthrough were muted by the absence of Russia.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Italian activist freed from Hungary returns home after being elected to European Parliament
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italian anti-fascist activist Ilaria Salis returned to her parents’ house in the northern Italian city of Monza on Saturday evening, after being freed from house arrest in Budapest the day before.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris announces $1.5B aid package for Ukraine at peace summit
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris announced a $1.5 billion aid package for Ukraine in its war against Russia at the Summit for Peace Saturday in Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Rome LGBTQ+ Pride parade celebrates 30th anniversary, makes fun of Pope Francis comments
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Rome LGBTQ+ Pride parade celebrated its 30th anniversary Saturday as tens of thousands of people in brightly colored outfits marched through the Italian capital waving banners, dancing and singing as they marked gay rights and poked fun at Pope Francis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US military strikes Houthi radar sites in Yemen after ship goes missing in Red Sea
item.description: The U.S. military is striking radar sites operated by Yemen’s Houthi rebels after a merchant sailor and boat went missing in the Red Sea earlier this week. U.S. strikes have destroyed seven radars within Houthi territory, the military’s Central Command (CENTCOM) said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: 8 Israeli soldiers killed in southern Gaza in deadliest attack on Israeli forces in months
item.description: Israel’s military says that eight soldiers have been killed in southern Gaza in the deadliest attack on Israeli forces in months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Princess Kate greets public for first time since cancer reveal
item.description: Kate, the Princess of Wales, took part in the annual "Trooping the Colour" ceremony Saturday to honor King Charles III's 75th birthday. The event, complete with a military parade, is her first public appearance since revealing her cancer diagnosis earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: Princess Kate makes her first public event since cancer diagnosis
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain put on a display of birthday pageantry Saturday for King Charles III, a military parade that marked the Princess of Wales ’ first appearance at a public event since her cancer diagnosis early this year.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Wind, rough seas again force US to temporarily relocate Gaza aid pier
item.description: The U.S. military once again is pausing operations on its temporary floating pier off the coast of Gaza as wind and rough seas are expected in the coming days. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Israeli-deployed AI in Gaza likely helps IDF reduce civilian casualties, expert says
item.description: The Israeli Defense Forces' use of AI has helped them to minimize civilian casualties while operating in a war zone where Hamas terrorists are located within and beneath civilian structures.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Muslim pilgrims converge at Mount Arafat for daylong worship as Hajj reaches its peak
item.description: More than 1.83 million Muslims from around the world gathered at the sacred hill of Mount Arafat in Saudi Arabia for worship and reflection on the second day of the Hajj pilgrimage.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: WSJ reporter Evan Gershkovich set to stand trial in Russia
item.description: Paul Beckett, assistant editor of the Wall Street Journal, weighs in on what it might take to bring reporter Evan Gershkovich home from Russia.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: US-built pier in Gaza is facing its latest challenge — whether the UN will keep delivering the aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S.-built pier to bring food to Gaza is facing one of its most serious challenges yet — its humanitarian partner is deciding if it can safely and ethically keep delivering supplies arriving by the U.S. sea route to starving Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Aides' vehicle in a convoy carrying Mexico's next president involved in a crash that killed 1
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A highway accident Friday involving an SUV carrying aides to Claudia Sheinbaum, who won Mexico’s June 2 presidential elections, has resulted in at least one fatality. Sheinbaum was not injured.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis warns of AI in first-ever G-7 papal address, urges 'safeguards' for 'proper human control'
item.description: Pope Francis warned world leaders attending the G-7 conference in Italy on Friday that artificial intelligence offers both immense benefit and serious ethical pitfalls for the future of humanity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cambodian authorities burn $70M of seized illegal drugs in major crackdown
item.description: Cambodian authorities on Friday destroyed more than seven tons of illicit drugs and the ingredients for them as a way to educate people about their danger.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico hot tub death: US tourist who was electrocuted remembered as ‘big family man’ who had 'heart of gold’
item.description: American tourist Jorge Guillen, who has died in Puerto Peñasco, Mexico after being electrocuted in a hot tub, is being remembered by those close to him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tourist electrocuted in hot tub at Mexican resort; officials blame wiring
item.description: A witness said she tried to enter the hot tub to check on the victim, but got a shock when she went in the water.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Luna, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 4 US college instructors stabbed in China
item.description: Four instructors from Iowa’s Cornell College who were teaching at a university in Jilin City, China, were stabbed Monday, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: What does Israel's rescue of 4 captives, and the killing of 274 Palestinians, mean for truce talks?
item.description: Israel’s dramatic rescue of four hostages from inside the Gaza Strip, in an operation that health officials say killed 274 Palestinians, comes at a sensitive time in the 8-month-old war.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Korea restarts anti-North Korea loudspeaker broadcasts in retaliation for trash balloons
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea on Sunday resumed anti-North Korean propaganda loudspeaker broadcasts in border areas in retaliation for the North sending over 1,000 balloons filled with trash and manure over the last couple of weeks.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9
